title: Zentauri UI — React component + hooks library with Tailwind CSS & motion
description: Zentauri UI is a React component + hooks library with Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion, and accessible primitives. Browse docs, interactive previews, and hooks to ship dashboards and marketing sites faster.

og.title: Zentauri UI — React components + hooks for modern apps
og.description: Minimalist React UI with Tailwind-first styling, motion presets, and copy-ready previews for Next.js and Vite.

twitter.title: Zentauri UI — React components + hooks for modern apps
twitter.description: Minimalist React UI with Tailwind-first styling, motion presets, and copy-ready previews for Next.js and Vite.

intro: A highly customizable component + hooks library built with Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion, focused on subtle interactions and a cohesive dark aesthetic. Use interactive previews and typed hooks to assemble production layouts without reinventing primitives.
useCases: Bootstrap dashboards with tables, navigation, overlays, and feedback components.
Prototype marketing flows with buttons, cards, accordions, and form controls.
Add motion-rich modals, drawers, tabs, and toasts with consistent animation tokens.
Document an internal design system with import paths that match published npm exports.
Extend Tailwind v4 themes while keeping class APIs predictable for contributors.

faqs.question: Is Zentauri UI only for Next.js?
faqs.answer: No. Components are React-first; the docs emphasize Next.js App Router patterns because they are common, but you can use the package with Vite, Remix, or other React 18+ setups that support Tailwind CSS v4.

faqs.question: Where do I install the package?
faqs.answer: Install @zentauri-ui/zentauri-components from npm, follow the Installation guide for Tailwind v4 scanning and peer dependencies, then import from the documented ui paths.

faqs.question: Are previews the same as production exports?
faqs.answer: Yes. Preview routes demonstrate the same exports you import in your app so you can copy snippets and align versions with your lockfile.

sections.heading: Why teams pick Zentauri UI
sections.body:
Zentauri UI pairs a tight visual language with practical defaults: glass and solid appearances, motion variants where they matter, and documentation that mirrors real import paths.

Instead of abstract galleries, you get per-component previews with code you can paste into a React or Next.js repo.

sections.heading: What ships today
sections.body:
The library covers layout, forms, overlays, data display, and feedback primitives—from buttons and inputs through modals, tables, and toasts—plus a growing set of hooks for window size, media queries, and other browser concerns.

Each family has its own preview route so you can compare density, animation, and accessibility notes before adopting a pattern org-wide.

sections.heading: Stack and compatibility
sections.body:
Components assume React 18+ and Tailwind CSS v4 with the published preset. Framer Motion backs animated variants; react-icons supplies consistent iconography in examples.

Keep peer installs aligned with the Installation page to avoid duplicate motion or merge conflicts in class composition.

sections.heading: How to explore the library
sections.body:
Start with Installation to wire Tailwind scanning and package aliases. Browse Components for UI families, Hooks for utilities, and use site search when you know a primitive name but not its route.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL in deployment so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve correctly for sharing.
